target.title: Fix OpenAI compaction runtime routing
target.body:
## Summary
- Route embedded OpenAI compaction through the selected runtime provider only when Codex auth is actually configured or explicitly selected.
- Use the same selected/persisted harness runtime for compaction auth routing and context-window sizing.
- Align local preflight compaction with configured OpenAI Responses server-compaction thresholds so replies do not stall on surprise local compaction below the server threshold.
- Add regression coverage for Codex OAuth compaction routing, direct OpenAI API-key preservation, persisted runtime context windows, post-compaction token state, and server-threshold gating.

## Root Cause
OpenAI model refs such as `openai/gpt-5.5` can run through the Codex harness, but the direct embedded compaction path still mixed raw model provider, selected runtime provider, and context-budget provider decisions. That let budget preflight compaction fall back from stale/missing native Codex thread binding into the context engine, then resolve auth as direct OpenAI API-key mode instead of the selected Codex OAuth route.

A second mismatch made the symptom much slower: local preflight compaction used `contextWindow - reserve - softThreshold` even when the model params configured OpenAI Responses server compaction at a higher threshold. In the observed local setup that caused preflight compaction around the mid-100k token range even though the configured server threshold was 200k, so Telegram ingress worked but replies appeared stuck before normal inference.

RCA proof:
- Before behavior: gateway logs showed Telegram ingress followed by native Codex compaction binding fallback, context-engine compaction, and previously `No_API_key_found_for_provider_openai` on the compaction path.
- Code evidence: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts` resolved compaction model/auth before consistently applying the selected OpenAI runtime provider, and its context-window lookup could use the current policy runtime instead of the persisted session runtime.
- Code evidence: `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` preflight gating ignored `responsesServerCompaction` / `responsesCompactThreshold` model params when deciding whether to run local compaction.
- Regression chain: recent compaction changes made required preflight compaction failures visible and made stale/missing native bindings fall through to context-engine compaction, exposing both the auth-route mismatch and the over-eager local preflight gate.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenAI GPT-5.5 compaction under Codex OAuth no longer asks for direct OpenAI API-key auth, direct OpenAI API-key configurations are preserved, and local preflight compaction waits for the configured server threshold instead of blocking replies early.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout and managed gateway on Linux, Node 25.9.0, configured default model `openai/gpt-5.5` with Codex runtime/OAuth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; `oc-update --skip-codex`; `pnpm openclaw status --all`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest reported `Test Files 4 passed (4)` and `Tests 131 passed (131)`; build completed successfully; `oc-update --skip-codex` rebuilt core/UI, reinstalled the daemon, and health check passed on retry; status reported Telegram `OK` and the gateway reachable.

Observed result after fix: the compaction unit path routes configured Codex OAuth sessions through `openai-codex`, keeps API-key-only OpenAI sessions on `openai`, uses the persisted Codex runtime for context-window sizing, and skips local preflight compaction below the configured OpenAI Responses server threshold.

What was not tested: no new live Telegram message was sent through the production topic after the patch; live status confirms Telegram/gateway readiness, while behavior proof is targeted unit coverage plus local build/update/status.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` found two accepted P2 issues; both were patched and covered by regression tests. Per user request, no further autoreview was run after the final API-key preservation patch.
- `oc-update --skip-codex`
- `pnpm openclaw status --all`

target.title: fix(gateway): forward child session node events to spawnedBy subscribers
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: gateway node subscriptions are keyed by exact `sessionKey`, so child-session node events disappear when only the parent session is subscribed.
- Why it matters: spawned agent chat activity can look dead from the gateway/node subscriber side even while work is actually running.
- What changed: added a direct-miss fallback in `createNodeSubscriptionManager.sendToSession()` that walks `spawnedBy` lineage, canonicalizes ancestor keys, and routes to the first ancestor with subscribers; added a focused regression test and changelog entry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change ACP execution, session transcript bridging, or broader subagent progress/status surfaces.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #34852
- Related #34905

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Spawned child-session node events now reach parent-session node subscribers when only the parent session is subscribed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway node subscriptions
- Relevant config (redacted): default test harness

### Steps

1. Subscribe a node to a parent session key.
2. Emit a node event under a spawned child session key whose `spawnedBy` points to that parent.
3. Observe delivery through the node subscription manager.

### Expected

- Parent subscriber receives the child-session event.

### Actual

- Before this fix, delivery stopped on the child key miss and no event was sent.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: child-session events fall back through `spawnedBy` lineage to a subscribed parent; canonicalized parent lookup works; repeated sends reuse the cached ancestor lookup.
- Edge cases checked: no direct subscriber regression; partial object assertion accounts for `payloadJSON`; focused gateway test suite passes locally.
- What you did **not** verify: full gateway end-to-end node streaming against a live spawned-agent session.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR to restore exact-key-only subscription routing.
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-node-subscriptions.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-misc.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: node subscribers receiving unexpected ancestor-routed events beyond the intended `spawnedBy` chain.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: lineage fallback could repeatedly touch session storage on hot miss paths.
  - Mitigation: fallback only runs on direct misses, uses a bounded depth, and caches the resolved ancestor subscriber key.

Supersedes the dirty branch state in #34905.

target.title: Policy: add secret and auth conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Policy 1.0 secret provenance and auth-profile conformance checks with redacted config evidence.

Logical base: merged #80783. This PR intentionally stays config-level only: it reads existing OpenClaw config and reports policy conformance findings through `doctor --lint`; it does not add runtime enforcement and it does not take on secrets management.

- Records managed SecretRef/provider provenance without storing secret values.
- Reports unmanaged SecretRefs, denied secret provider sources, insecure secret provider posture, missing auth profile metadata, and unapproved auth profile modes.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and evidence shape.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "secrets": {
    "requireManagedProviders": true,
    "denySources": ["exec"],
    "allowInsecureProviders": false
  },
  "auth": {
    "profiles": {
      "requireMetadata": ["provider", "mode"],
      "allowModes": ["api_key", "token"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Safety

This is policy conformance, not secrets regulation or storage management. `doctor --lint` reads config-level declarations, emits redacted findings, and leaves credential stores and runtime behavior to the existing auth/credential systems.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: config-level Policy conformance for redacted secret provenance and auth-profile metadata/mode rules.

Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-81974-policy` on Ubuntu-24.04; PR head `d72baa8115b12041a1c532479e919480c46eda57` is signed and GitHub-verified.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git push fork policy-81974-config:policy-secrets-auth-provenance`
- `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-81974/openclaw.json timeout 180 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --severity-min error`
- Earlier same-head verification: `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
- Earlier same-head verification: `git diff --check`
- Earlier same-head verification: `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- Earlier same-head verification: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot`
- Earlier same-head verification: `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-81974-original-lint.tsbuildinfo`
- Earlier same-head verification: autoreview local pass #1 and #2 clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Evidence after fix: the real `doctor --lint` CLI run loaded a temp workspace policy and config, ran 37 checks, and returned four redacted policy errors:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 37,
  "checksSkipped": 0,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/auth-profile-unapproved-mode",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/auth/profiles/oauth",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/auth/profiles/allowModes",
      "message": "Auth profile 'oauth' uses mode 'oauth' outside the policy allowlist."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-denied-provider-source",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/secrets/providers/command",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/denySources",
      "message": "Secret provider 'command' uses denied source 'exec'."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-insecure-provider",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/secrets/providers/vault",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/allowInsecureProviders",
      "message": "Secret provider 'vault' enables insecure posture: allowInsecurePath."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-unmanaged-provider",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/models/providers/anthropic/apiKey",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/requireManagedProviders",
      "message": "SecretRef uses unmanaged provider 'missing'."
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: the CLI reported the expected secret/auth conformance findings through `doctor --lint` without printing private secret values.

What was not tested: no live Gateway was launched; this is config-level policy conformance proof only.

## Related

- #80783 - Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
- #81981 - Policy: add gateway exposure checks

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.

target.title: security(ci): mask derived base64 auth header in custom checkout blocks
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(): beta blocker - ` and link the matching `Beta blocker: - ` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation. - Problem: The inline checkout scripts in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` build a Basic-auth header as `base64("x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}")` but never register the derived value with the runner's log scrubber. GitHub Actions only auto-masks the raw `GITHUB_TOKEN`, not values derived from it. - Why it matters: If any downstream step runs under `set -x`, emits verbose `git` tracing, or fails while printing the fetch command, the base64 blob can surface in public CI logs and be trivially decoded back to a live installation token. - What changed: Added `echo "::add-mask::${auth_header}"` immediately after the header is constructed in every custom checkout block (11 jobs), so the runner redacts the value from all subsequent log output. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to job graph, matrices, timeouts, permissions, triggers, runners, or any non-checkout step. Behavior is identical; only the logging surface is hardened. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [x] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: The custom checkout scripts bypass `actions/checkout` and construct their own `AUTHORIZATION: basic ` header. The runner's automatic secret masking only covers the literal `GITHUB_TOKEN` string, so the base64-encoded derivative is unprotected in logs. - Missing detection / guardrail: No lint or workflow audit rule flags derived-secret values that are not passed through `::add-mask::` before use. - Contributing context (if known): Multiple jobs duplicate the same checkout block, so the gap is replicated across every lane that uses it. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`security-scm-fast` job, zizmor audit step) - Scenario the test should lock in: Any base64-encoded auth header built inline must be passed to `::add-mask::` before the first step that could emit it to logs. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: zizmor already runs on changed workflow files in CI and is the canonical static auditor for this class of issue. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `security-scm-fast` runs zizmor on changed workflows. - If no new test is added, why not: The change is a one-line log-scrubber registration with no runtime behavior to assert; static workflow audit is the correct layer. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes None. ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text Before: [build auth_header=base64(token)] -> [git fetch ... AUTHORIZATION: basic $auth_header] -> [on verbose/failure: header visible in logs] After: [build auth_header=base64(token)] -> [::add-mask:: auth_header] -> [git fetch ...] -> [runner scrubs value from all logs] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Risk — a base64-encoded GitHub token derivative could leak via verbose shell traces, git debug output, or error paths that echo the fetch command. Mitigation — register the derived header with the runner's log scrubber via `::add-mask::` in every job that constructs it, so any accidental emission is redacted before reaching the log stream. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (GitHub-hosted / Blacksmith runners per existing `runs-on` matrix) - Runtime/container: default runner image, no container - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): `.github/workflows/ci.yml` ### Steps 1. Push the branch and open this PR so the `CI` workflow runs. 2. Open any job that uses the custom checkout block (e.g. `build-artifacts`, `checks-fast-core`). 3. Inspect the raw log of the `Checkout` step and confirm no base64 `AUTHORIZATION` value is emitted; the existing `zizmor` audit on changed workflows passes. ### Expected - All jobs pass with identical behavior to `main`; any log line that would have contained the base64 header shows `***` instead. ### Actual - Matches expected on the pushed branch. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Demonstration run on my fork showing that `::add-mask::` scrubs a base64 `x-access-token:` header from GitHub Actions logs (dummy value, no real secret involved): - Without mask: `header value is eC1hY2Nlc3MtdG9rZW46ZGVtby12YWx1ZS1ub3QtYS1zZWNyZXQ=` - After `::add-mask::`: `header value is ***` Full run: https://github.com/anurag-bg-neu/openclaw/actions/runs/24701668471/job/72245961669 ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Confirmed the `::add-mask::` line is present in every custom checkout block that constructs `auth_header` (11 occurrences), placed immediately after header construction and before the first `git` invocation. - Edge cases checked: No shell quoting or escaping change; `auth_header` is still expanded the same way in the `http.extraheader` config. Retry loop and checkout semantics unchanged. - What you did **not** verify: Runtime behavior on the Blacksmith self-hosted runner class — indistinguishable from GitHub-hosted since `::add-mask::` is a runner workflow command, not a runner-class feature. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: None beyond the zero-behavior-change surface; `::add-mask::` is a log-scrubber directive and does not alter execution. - Mitigation: Existing `security-scm-fast` zizmor audit continues to gate workflow changes.

target.title: fix(cron): make isolated agent watchdogs configurable
target.body:
## Summary
- raises isolated-agent cron setup/pre-execution watchdog defaults from 60s to 3m
- adds `cron.isolatedAgentSetupWatchdog` and `cron.isolatedAgentPreExecutionWatchdog` config fields
- documents the new knobs and adds schema/timer regression coverage

## Why
Some isolated agent cron jobs can spend more than 60s in provider/runtime startup before first execution progress, especially on loaded hosts or with larger bootstraps. The previous hardcoded watchdog could abort these jobs at the pre-execution dispatch phase even when direct agent execution is healthy.

## Safety
- existing configs continue to work
- setup watchdog remains separate from the execution timeout budget so queued runner-lane time does not spend `timeoutSeconds`
- pre-execution watchdog remains capped to half of the job timeout
- invalid non-positive duration config is rejected by schema validation
- failure diagnostics remain phase-specific

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated `agentTurn` cron jobs could fail during setup or pre-execution startup at the old hardcoded 60s watchdog, even when the underlying agent execution was otherwise healthy.
- **Real environment tested:** Corroborating live proof from @kip-claw on a self-hosted Raspberry Pi 5 (4 GB, ARM64), Debian bookworm, OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (`f066dd2`), systemd service, OAuth via `openai-codex`, `openai/gpt-5.4` primary with `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` fallback. See Kip's PR comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82223#issuecomment-4470372274
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Kip reproduced the failure with 19 isolated cron jobs, patched both built cron watchdog constants from `60000` to `180000`, cleared Node compile cache, restarted Gateway, and reran the affected isolated cron class. This PR matches that observed fix by making both setup and pre-execution watchdog defaults `3m` through typed source/config/docs coverage.
- **Evidence after fix:** Kip's redacted live output shows the representative run progressing beyond the old 60s watchdog and completing:

```text
06:40:38.036  lane dequeue
06:40:50.488  session state -> processing (runner started, 12s)
06:42:48.542  session state -> idle (run_completed)
06:42:48.622  embedded run done: durationMs=130542 aborted=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The Pi hardware health check job changed from `error` to `ok` and completed successfully in about 130s with `aborted=false`, which is inside the new 180s default setup/pre-execution watchdog budget.
- **What was not tested:** No production cron cutover, public side effects, or unrelated cron lanes were exercised by this PR proof. The exact PR-head source/config path is covered by schema and timer regression tests; Kip's live proof validates the same 180s runtime behavior in a real OpenClaw deployment.

Additional redacted local Gateway proof from a real OpenClaw setup:

Before the watchdog change, representative isolated agent cron runs failed during startup/pre-execution cleanup with the phase-specific diagnostic below, even though direct agent execution was healthy:

```text
status: error
error: cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start (last phase: attempt-dispatch)
durationMs: ~60000
```

After applying the equivalent local runtime change to use a longer isolated-agent setup/pre-execution watchdog and restarting the Gateway, the same representative isolated cron class completed instead of being aborted at the old 60s ceiling:

```text
status: ok
error: null
startup/pre-execution time: >60000ms and <300000ms
```

This PR turns that local recovery into typed config, schema validation, docs, and regression coverage rather than keeping a dist-only local patch. The public evidence above is intentionally redacted to avoid exposing local paths, private job names, or private network details.

## Test plan
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [ ] Local targeted Vitest rerun after matching Kip's 180s proof is pending CI or a dependency-restored checkout; this clean local checkout has no `node_modules`, so `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` cannot resolve `vitest/package.json` without installing dependencies.

target.title: feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks
target.body:
# PR Draft — #70976 model failover hooks

## Title
`feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks`

## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw exposes model failover and terminal all-models-failed outcomes mostly through logs, so plugins and operators cannot react in-process without log scraping.
- Why it matters: This makes alerting and automation brittle, delayed, and hard to build on top of the existing failover path.
- What changed: Added two fire-and-forget plugin hooks, `model_failover` and `model_failure_terminal`, and emitted them from the existing failover-decision and terminal before-reply failure seams.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add non-verbose fallback notices, workspace-context re-injection, or circuit-breaker/policy behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #70976
- Related #65824
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Plugins can now register `model_failover` hooks and receive structured failover decision events.
- Plugins can now register `model_failure_terminal` hooks and receive structured before-reply terminal failure events, including fallback-attempt summaries for all-models-failed outcomes.
- No default user-facing fallback messaging behavior changed.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[model error] -> [log failover decision] -> [fallback or final failure]
                     \-> plugins must scrape logs

After:
[model error] -> [log failover decision + model_failover hook] -> [fallback or final failure]
                                                               \-> [model_failure_terminal hook on before-reply collapse]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds new plugin observability hooks. To reduce leakage risk, `model_failure_terminal` is gated by the existing conversation-access policy for non-bundled plugins, and attempt summaries expose provider/model/reason rather than raw provider error blobs.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A (hook plumbing)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test config

### Steps

1. Register a plugin hook for `model_failover` or `model_failure_terminal` using the hook runner test helpers.
2. Trigger the corresponding hook runner path or failover seam.
3. Verify the handler receives the structured event payload and that hook failures do not break the main flow.

### Expected

- `model_failover` fires on failover decisions.
- `model_failure_terminal` fires on terminal before-reply failures.
- Hook execution is fire-and-forget / fail-open.

### Actual

- Added targeted tests for both hook types and failover-observation hook emission.
- Full plugin Vitest suite passed after the change.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `model_failover` emits structured fallback telemetry from the embedded failover decision seam, and `model_failure_terminal` is blocked for non-bundled plugins unless conversation access is explicitly enabled.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS / Node 25.6.0, using the production plugin registry, production hook runner, and production embedded failover decision logger from this branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx .artifacts/oc-70990-real-proof.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
[agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision: runId=proof-run-1 stage=prompt decision=fallback_model reason=rate_limit from=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 to=openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 profile=-
{
  "diagnostics": [
    {
      "level": "warn",
      "pluginId": "proof-terminal-blocked",
      "message": "typed hook "model_failure_terminal" blocked because non-bundled plugins must set plugins.entries.proof-terminal-blocked.hooks.allowConversationAccess=true"
    }
  ],
  "hasHooks": {
    "model_failover": true,
    "model_failure_terminal": true
  },
  "eventCount": 2,
  "events": [
    {
      "hook": "model_failover",
      "event": {
        "runId": "proof-run-1",
        "agentId": "proof-agent",
        "sessionId": "proof-session-id",
        "sessionKey": "agent:proof:direct:1",
        "provider": "openrouter",
        "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
        "sourceProvider": "openai-codex",
        "sourceModel": "gpt-5.5",
        "stage": "prompt",
        "decision": "fallback_model",
        "failoverReason": "rate_limit",
        "profileFailureReason": null,
        "fallbackConfigured": true,
        "status": 429,
        "sourceRecoverable": true
      }
    },
    {
      "hook": "model_failure_terminal",
      "event": {
        "runId": "proof-run-1",
        "agentId": "proof-agent",
        "sessionId": "proof-session-id",
        "sessionKey": "agent:proof:direct:1",
        "finalMessage": "All configured models failed before reply.",
        "kind": "all_models_failed",
        "attempts": [
          { "provider": "openai-codex", "model": "gpt-5.5", "reason": "rate_limit" },
          { "provider": "openrouter", "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6", "reason": "overloaded" }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The production registry rejected an ungated non-bundled `model_failure_terminal` registration, accepted the gated terminal hook, emitted one `model_failover` event with failed source refs (`sourceProvider` / `sourceModel`) and the selected fallback target (`provider` / `model`) plus `rate_limit`, `status: 429`, and `sourceRecoverable: true`, and emitted one `model_failure_terminal` event with all-models-failed attempt summaries.
- **What was not tested**: A live external provider outage was not forced; this proof exercises the local OpenClaw plugin registry/hook runner/failover-observation path without making network calls.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - reviewed the source seams where failover decisions and terminal failures are emitted
  - verified the new hook names are registered in the plugin hook surface
  - verified targeted tests pass for runner plumbing and failover-observation emission
  - verified the full plugin Vitest suite passes
  - verified the selected fallback target is published separately from failed source refs
  - verified `pnpm check:changed` passes locally on the current head
- Edge cases checked:
  - no-op when no hook is registered
  - fail-open behavior when a hook throws
  - repeated decision emissions still invoke hooks correctly
- What you did **not** verify:
  - a live end-to-end provider outage scenario outside the test suite

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook payload scope may be seen as too narrow for v1 because it omits richer source-model context.
  - Mitigation: Keep this PR intentionally minimal and source-backed; richer payload fields can be added in follow-up work if maintainers want them.
- Risk: Hook emission could accidentally interfere with failover paths if blocking.
  - Mitigation: Both hooks are wired through existing fire-and-forget `runVoidHook` behavior.
- Risk: Terminal failure event content could leak too much provider detail.
  - Mitigation: `model_failure_terminal` uses the sanitized message path and structured attempt summaries instead of raw provider error blobs.




## Latest local validation

Current head: `cdfe51e6d8d51bc6dcc66bad2274b07b1ebdaa46`

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts` — passed (`28` tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader.test.ts` — passed (`131` tests)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-observation.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts` — passed
- `CI=true pnpm check:changed` — passed
- `node --import tsx .artifacts/oc-70990-real-proof.ts` — passed; proof output now shows `from=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 to=openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` and the hook event target as `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`.

target.title: fix(memory-core): keep dreaming private store off workspace root [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Move memory-core dreaming private JSON state writes from the workspace root store to a store rooted at `memory/.dreams`.
- Keep `memory/.dreams` private while avoiding `chmod 0700` on the user-facing workspace root and `memory/` parent directory.
- Add regression coverage showing workspace and `memory/` modes are preserved while private artifacts remain restricted.

Refs #56263; this fixes the memory-core dreaming private-store manifestation described in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56263#issuecomment-4520113754 without claiming to resolve the broader permission-policy issue.

## Why

`privateFileStore(workspaceDir).writeJson("memory/.dreams/...")` delegates to fs-safe private writes with the workspace as the private store root. fs-safe then hardens the store root and nested parent directories to `0700`, which can collapse POSIX ACL masks on operator-managed shared workspaces.

For dreaming internals, only `memory/.dreams` needs to be the private artifact root. Rooting the private store there keeps the sensitive artifacts restricted without mutating broader workspace permissions.

## Real Behavior Proof


- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Memory-core dreaming private JSON writes should no longer chmod the shared workspace root or `memory/` directory to `0700`; only `memory/.dreams` and files below it should be private.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw workspace bind-mounted through `/home/shared/workspace` with POSIX ACLs granting `www-data` access for WebDAV.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the patched `writeDreamingPrivateJson("/home/shared/workspace", "memory/.dreams/pr85435-after-fix-proof.json", ...)` helper through `pnpm exec tsx --tsconfig tsconfig.json` against the live shared workspace path.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the live run showed `/home/shared/workspace` and `/home/shared/workspace/memory` stayed `770` with `user:www-data:rwx` and `mask::rwx` after the patched write, while `memory/.dreams` became `700` and the proof JSON became `600`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched path preserved the WebDAV-facing ACL mask on the workspace root and `memory/` parent directory; only the intended private artifact directory was hardened. The temporary proof JSON was removed after the check.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run locally because this PR is intentionally narrow; targeted memory-core test, shrinkwrap check, and whitespace check were run instead.

### Before this patch, live reproduction on Linux

Setup: a shared workspace directory with POSIX ACL access for a WebDAV service user.

Before writing through the private file store:

```text
drwxrwx--- 770 /path/to/shared/workspace
drwxrwx--- 770 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory
drwxrwx--- 770 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx
```

Trigger:

```js
import { fileStore } from "@openclaw/fs-safe/store";

const store = fileStore({ rootDir: "/path/to/shared/workspace", private: true });
await store.writeJson("memory/.dreams/repro-private-file-store.json", { ok: true });
```

Observed result before the fix:

```text
drwx------ 700 /path/to/shared/workspace
drwx------ 700 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory
drwx------ 700 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
user:www-data:rwx    #effective:---
group::rwx           #effective:---
mask::---
```

A syscall probe caught the chmods from Node's libuv worker:

```text
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=700 path=/path/to/shared/workspace
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=700 path=/path/to/shared/workspace/memory
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=700 path=/path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=600 path=/path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams/repro-private-file-store...
```

### After this patch, live Linux ACL validation

Command used against the same shared WebDAV-backed workspace shape, via the patched memory-core helper:

```text
pnpm exec tsx --tsconfig tsconfig.json - <<'TS'
import { writeDreamingPrivateJson } from "./extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.ts";
await writeDreamingPrivateJson("/home/shared/workspace", "memory/.dreams/pr85435-after-fix-proof.json", { ok: true });
TS
```

Observed before the patched write:

```text
/home/shared/workspace 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx
```

Observed after the patched write:

```text
/home/shared/workspace 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams 700 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams/pr85435-after-fix-proof.json 600 lagoon3:lagoon3

# workspace and memory ACLs stayed usable:
/path/to/shared/workspace
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx

/path/to/shared/workspace/memory
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx

# only the private artifact directory was hardened:
/path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
user:www-data:rwx    #effective:---
mask::---
```

The temporary proof JSON was removed after the check.

### After this patch, local targeted validation

Command:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.test.ts
```

Output:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       4 passed (4)
```

The regression tests verify:

- workspace root remains `0770`
- `memory/` remains `0770`
- `memory/.dreams` is private `0700`
- written JSON artifact is private `0600`
- paths outside `memory/.dreams` are rejected by the helper
- symlinked `memory` and symlinked `.dreams` paths are rejected before the private store root is used

Additional targeted checks:

```text
pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check
# all generated npm-shrinkwrap.json files are current

git diff --check
# no whitespace errors
```

## Not Run

- Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run; this PR is scoped to memory-core private dreaming artifact storage and I used targeted tests only.
- `codex review --base origin/main` was not run locally.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted change. I reviewed the code path, reproduced the issue on a local Linux setup, and kept the patch narrowly scoped to memory-core dreaming private artifacts.

target.title: refactor(agents): carry resolved model runtime through auth
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (@osolmaz). Ready for maintainer review.

AI-assisted implementation requested by a maintainer.

## Summary

Carry the resolved model runtime through the embedded agent path.

This is a follow-up to #80488. That PR fixed the narrow transport-aware per-model override path; this PR handles the more general runtime/plugin-owned path by keeping the selected provider, final model API/base URL, provider refs that own auth/stream hooks, and runtime plan together after model resolution. Later auth, stream setup, compaction, and runtime planning use those resolved facts instead of deriving them again from provider/model strings.

Refs #80487. Follow-up to #80488.

## What Changed

- Added a resolved model runtime descriptor with provider/model ref, effective transport, auth provider refs, and source metadata.
- Updated sync and async model resolution to return that descriptor alongside the existing resolved model.
- Passed resolved runtime auth facts into embedded-run and compaction credential resolution, synthetic provider auth, and synthetic-profile defer hooks.
- Let shared model auth derive selected-model auth facts when a caller has only the resolved model and not an explicit descriptor.
- Let provider runtime auth hooks use explicit resolved provider refs when available.
- Built the agent runtime plan from the resolved descriptor after runtime model updates.
- Routed stream hook lookup through the selected model API owner when a custom provider's selected model changes API, and made Ollama selected-model `baseUrl` override provider `baseUrl`.

## Testing

Latest after rebasing onto current `upstream/main`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts extensions/ollama/index.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.synthetic-auth-discovery.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts` - passed, 14 files / 510 tests.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts` - passed, 0 runtime value cycles.

Earlier validation on the same implementation before the final rebase:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts` - passed, 4 files / 200 tests after the shared-auth fallback and compaction follow-through fix.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/ollama/index.test.ts extensions/ollama/index.ts extensions/ollama/provider-discovery.ts extensions/ollama/src/discovery-shared.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.harness.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/resolved-model-runtime.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.ts src/agents/resolved-model-runtime.types.ts src/plugins/provider-external-auth.types.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/types.ts` - passed.
- `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen2.5:3b OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_WEB_SEARCH=0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.live.config.ts extensions/ollama/ollama.live.test.ts -t "runs native chat"` - passed, 1 live test / 3 skipped by filter.
- Manual local source CLI proof with a custom provider whose provider-level API/baseUrl points at an inert router and whose selected model overrides to native local Ollama - passed.
- `codex review --base upstream/main` - passed after fixing the review finding about callers that resolve model auth without an explicit runtime descriptor.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: custom/router-backed model selections now carry the final resolved transport/auth/stream facts into auth, stream setup, compaction, and runtime planning. A provider default no longer stands in for a per-model runtime choice.

Real environment tested: local source checkout with Node 22, repo dependencies installed, and local Ollama serving `qwen2.5:3b` at `http://127.0.0.1:11434`. No external provider secrets were printed or committed.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: created a temporary `openclaw.json` where `models.providers.custom-router` had provider-level `api: "openai-completions"` and `baseUrl: "https://router.invalid/v1"`, while selected model `custom-router/qwen2.5:3b` had model-level `api: "ollama"` and `baseUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:11434"`; then ran:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmp/state" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmp/openclaw.json" \
OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 \
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA=1 \
OLLAMA_API_KEY= \
node scripts/run-node.mjs infer model run \
  --local \
  --model custom-router/qwen2.5:3b \
  --prompt 'Reply with exactly one word: pong' \
  --json
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "model.run",
  "transport": "local",
  "provider": "custom-router",
  "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "text": "pong",
      "mediaUrl": null
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw kept the visible selected provider/model as `custom-router/qwen2.5:3b`, but used the selected model's native Ollama API/base URL for the actual local run. The inert provider-level router URL was not used, and the command returned `ok: true` with the local model response.

What was not tested: full remote `pnpm check:changed` proof is blocked in this environment because Blacksmith Testbox is unavailable (`blacksmith` CLI missing) and direct AWS Crabbox has no usable AWS credentials/IMDS identity. Live Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI auth-profile smokes were not run in this local pass.

## Risks

- This is an internal descriptor plumbing change, so the main risk is stale callers continuing to infer runtime facts from only provider/model strings.
- Plugin auth hook contexts receive additive optional fields only, but this is still public plugin contract surface and should get owner review before merge.

- Repair iOS QR/manual gateway pairing state so new setup codes replace stale auth immediately, and allow provider auth profile resolution to fall back through API key and OAuth-backed OpenAI profiles for realtime.

target.title: fix(memory-core): keep dreaming private store off workspace root [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Move memory-core dreaming private JSON state writes from the workspace root store to a store rooted at `memory/.dreams`.
- Keep `memory/.dreams` private while avoiding `chmod 0700` on the user-facing workspace root and `memory/` parent directory.
- Add regression coverage showing workspace and `memory/` modes are preserved while private artifacts remain restricted.

Refs #56263; this fixes the memory-core dreaming private-store manifestation described in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56263#issuecomment-4520113754 without claiming to resolve the broader permission-policy issue.

## Why

`privateFileStore(workspaceDir).writeJson("memory/.dreams/...")` delegates to fs-safe private writes with the workspace as the private store root. fs-safe then hardens the store root and nested parent directories to `0700`, which can collapse POSIX ACL masks on operator-managed shared workspaces.

For dreaming internals, only `memory/.dreams` needs to be the private artifact root. Rooting the private store there keeps the sensitive artifacts restricted without mutating broader workspace permissions.

## Real Behavior Proof


- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Memory-core dreaming private JSON writes should no longer chmod the shared workspace root or `memory/` directory to `0700`; only `memory/.dreams` and files below it should be private.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux OpenClaw workspace bind-mounted through `/home/shared/workspace` with POSIX ACLs granting `www-data` access for WebDAV.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the patched `writeDreamingPrivateJson("/home/shared/workspace", "memory/.dreams/pr85435-after-fix-proof.json", ...)` helper through `pnpm exec tsx --tsconfig tsconfig.json` against the live shared workspace path.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the live run showed `/home/shared/workspace` and `/home/shared/workspace/memory` stayed `770` with `user:www-data:rwx` and `mask::rwx` after the patched write, while `memory/.dreams` became `700` and the proof JSON became `600`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The patched path preserved the WebDAV-facing ACL mask on the workspace root and `memory/` parent directory; only the intended private artifact directory was hardened. The temporary proof JSON was removed after the check.
- **What was not tested:** Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run locally because this PR is intentionally narrow; targeted memory-core test, shrinkwrap check, and whitespace check were run instead.

### Before this patch, live reproduction on Linux

Setup: a shared workspace directory with POSIX ACL access for a WebDAV service user.

Before writing through the private file store:

```text
drwxrwx--- 770 /path/to/shared/workspace
drwxrwx--- 770 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory
drwxrwx--- 770 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx
```

Trigger:

```js
import { fileStore } from "@openclaw/fs-safe/store";

const store = fileStore({ rootDir: "/path/to/shared/workspace", private: true });
await store.writeJson("memory/.dreams/repro-private-file-store.json", { ok: true });
```

Observed result before the fix:

```text
drwx------ 700 /path/to/shared/workspace
drwx------ 700 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory
drwx------ 700 /path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
user:www-data:rwx    #effective:---
group::rwx           #effective:---
mask::---
```

A syscall probe caught the chmods from Node's libuv worker:

```text
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=700 path=/path/to/shared/workspace
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=700 path=/path/to/shared/workspace/memory
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=700 path=/path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
chmod comm=libuv-worker mode=600 path=/path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams/repro-private-file-store...
```

### After this patch, live Linux ACL validation

Command used against the same shared WebDAV-backed workspace shape, via the patched memory-core helper:

```text
pnpm exec tsx --tsconfig tsconfig.json - <<'TS'
import { writeDreamingPrivateJson } from "./extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.ts";
await writeDreamingPrivateJson("/home/shared/workspace", "memory/.dreams/pr85435-after-fix-proof.json", { ok: true });
TS
```

Observed before the patched write:

```text
/home/shared/workspace 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx
```

Observed after the patched write:

```text
/home/shared/workspace 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory 770 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams 700 lagoon3:lagoon3
/home/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams/pr85435-after-fix-proof.json 600 lagoon3:lagoon3

# workspace and memory ACLs stayed usable:
/path/to/shared/workspace
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx

/path/to/shared/workspace/memory
user:www-data:rwx
mask::rwx

# only the private artifact directory was hardened:
/path/to/shared/workspace/memory/.dreams
user:www-data:rwx    #effective:---
mask::---
```

The temporary proof JSON was removed after the check.

### After this patch, local targeted validation

Command:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-private-store.test.ts
```

Output:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       4 passed (4)
```

The regression tests verify:

- workspace root remains `0770`
- `memory/` remains `0770`
- `memory/.dreams` is private `0700`
- written JSON artifact is private `0600`
- paths outside `memory/.dreams` are rejected by the helper
- symlinked `memory` and symlinked `.dreams` paths are rejected before the private store root is used

Additional targeted checks:

```text
pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check
# all generated npm-shrinkwrap.json files are current

git diff --check
# no whitespace errors
```

## Not Run

- Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run; this PR is scoped to memory-core private dreaming artifact storage and I used targeted tests only.
- `codex review --base origin/main` was not run locally.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted change. I reviewed the code path, reproduced the issue on a local Linux setup, and kept the patch narrowly scoped to memory-core dreaming private artifacts.

target.title: fix(twitch): preserve newer message handler during cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83888. `TwitchClientManager.onMessage` cleanup unconditionally deleted the stored handler by key. If a second `onMessage` for the same account replaced the handler, running the first cleanup deleted the newer handler, silently dropping all inbound messages. The cleanup now removes the handler only if it is still the one it registered.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: the cleanup closure returned by `onMessage` called `messageHandlers.delete(key)` unconditionally; a later `onMessage` for the same account installs a newer handler, and running the earlier cleanup then evicted that newer handler, leaving the account with no handler and silently dropping inbound messages. The cleanup now guards with a referential check so it only removes the handler it registered.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Ran the Twitch extension test suite via the repo's vitest runner against the real TwitchClientManager.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the onMessage tests on both the pre-fix and post-fix code through the real runtime.

Evidence after fix: stdout from the vitest run against the real runtime, comparing the pre-fix (buggy) and post-fix code:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/twitch/src/twitch-client.test.ts -t onMessage

-- BEFORE the fix (unconditional delete): the regression test fails --
  x cleanup of an earlier handler does not remove a newer registered handler (83888)
    AssertionError: expected undefined to be [Function Mock]
    messageHandlers.get(key) returned undefined; handler2 was wrongly evicted
  Test Files  1 failed (1)

-- AFTER the fix (referential guard): all onMessage tests pass --
  + should register message handler for account
  + should replace existing handler for same account
  + cleanup of an earlier handler does not remove a newer registered handler (83888)
  + cleanup of the current handler removes it
  Tests  4 passed | 29 skipped (33)
```

Observed result after fix: before the fix the regression test fails because the earlier cleanup evicts the newer handler (the stored handler becomes undefined); after the fix the newer handler remains registered and cleanup of the current handler still removes it. The full Twitch extension suite (14 files, 169 tests) passes unchanged.

What was not tested: a live Twitch chat session was not run; the handler lifecycle is exercised through the real TwitchClientManager under vitest, and the before/after comparison demonstrates the regression test captures the exact defect.

target.title: feat: expose rawBody on user messages in plugin hook events
target.body:
### Summary

Add `rawBody` field to user messages in `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`, and `before_agent_start` (legacy) hook events. This provides plugins with the original user input before channel structural context, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` metadata, and prompt-build hook injections are prepended.

### Motivation

Plugin hooks receive `event.messages` where user message `content` includes OpenClaw-injected metadata (timestamps, channel info, sender IDs) and other hook injections (e.g., memory context). Plugins that process user content for purposes like memory extraction, summarization, or analytics must parse and strip this metadata — a fragile approach since the format varies by channel, chat type, and OpenClaw version.

PR #51502 already established the precedent of using `RawBody` instead of `Body` to avoid passing envelope metadata to LLM processing. This PR extends that principle to plugin hook events.

### Changes

- Compute `rawBodyForHooks` from `CommandBody ?? RawBody ?? Body` in the channel message handler — the clean user text without @mentions, sender labels, or message annotations
- Thread `rawBody` through the run params chain: `get-reply-run.ts` → `runReplyAgent` → `runAgentTurnWithFallback` → `runEmbeddedPiAgent` → `runEmbeddedAttempt`
- Include `rawBody` in hook event objects for `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end`
- Add `rawBody` to `FollowupRun` type so the value survives queueing/deferred runs
- `rawBody` is `undefined` for non-channel runs (CLI, heartbeat, cron)
- **Steer race fix** (`86ebda8b`): Thread `rawBody` through the steer queue path so steered injections on non-Telegram channels refresh the active embedded run's hook context

### Backward Compatibility

- Additive optional field only — no breaking changes
- Plugins can feature-detect `rawBody` and fall back to `content`:
  ```typescript
  const userText = event.rawBody ?? extractText(lastUser.content);
  ```
- Plugin SDK API baseline unchanged (no drift)

### Use Case

Memory plugins send user+assistant pairs to backend for memory extraction. Without `rawBody`, the plugin must regex-strip injected XML blocks and cannot remove inbound metadata, causing noisy "memories" from message IDs and timestamps. With `rawBody`, the plugin gets clean user input directly.

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin hook events (`before_prompt_build`, `agent_end`) now expose `event.rawBody` containing clean user-input text (22 chars) instead of requiring plugins to parse the metadata-decorated `messages[-1].content` (411 chars). Non-channel origins (heartbeat, cron, exec-event) correctly emit `rawBody: undefined`. Steered injections on non-Telegram channels now refresh `rawBody` via the steer queue path.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.17.0-1013-aws (arm64), Node v22.22.2, OpenClaw 2026.5.20, Bedrock `global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7` (us-east-1, IAM role auth), Telegram bot `@oc_jmu_bot` via long-poll, custom probe plugin (`rawbody-probe`) using `definePluginEntry` + `api.on("before_prompt_build")` + `api.on("agent_end")`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built from PR HEAD (`git rev-parse HEAD` = `29e89cfb84`), ran `pnpm build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway --allow-unconfigured --port 18799 --bind loopback --auth none --verbose`. Sent Telegram DM "hello rawbody test 004" to `@oc_jmu_bot`. Triggered heartbeat via `node openclaw.mjs system event --text "..." --mode now --url ws://127.0.0.1:18799 --token anonymous --json`. Ran unit tests: `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts -t "rawBody hook gating" --reporter=verbose`.

Evidence after fix:
```

Case B — Heartbeat origin:

Unit tests after fix (6/6 pass):
✓ passes user text as rawBody for telegram channel runs       (62ms)
✓ gates rawBody to undefined for heartbeat runs               (2ms)
✓ gates rawBody to undefined for cron-event runs              (1ms)
✓ gates rawBody to undefined for exec-event runs              (1ms)
✓ prefers CommandBody over Body for channel rawBody           (1ms)
✓ threads rawBody through steer path for non-telegram channels (3ms)
Test Files  1 passed

```

Observed result after fix: `event.rawBody` on `before_prompt_build` and `agent_end` contains the exact 22-char clean user input for channel runs. Non-channel origins correctly emit `undefined`. The steer path unit test confirms `followupRun.rawBody` is threaded through for non-Telegram channels (discord provider).

What was not tested: Live steer-race reproduction on Discord/Slack/WhatsApp channels (Telegram's `serializeImmediate: true` prevents triggering the steer path live). target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
Closes #79588

## Problem

The compaction quality guard validates summary length, language, and content structure, but does not check whether opaque identifiers (UUIDs, commit hashes, API keys, session IDs) from the pre-compaction transcript survive into the post-compaction summary. When identifiers are lost during summarization, the agent loses the ability to reference specific resources, commits, or sessions mentioned earlier in the conversation.

## What changed

### `compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` (new functions)

- **`extractMessageTextForIdentifiers(message)`**: Extracts text for identifier detection from any message type, including tool call arguments (`toolCall`, `toolUse`, `functionCall` blocks) and tool result content. Skips `toolResult.details` (stripped for security).
- **`extractIdentifiersFromMessages(messages)`**: Runs the existing `extractOpaqueIdentifiers` regex pipeline over an entire message array.
- **`computeLostIdentifiers(sourceIdentifiers, summary)`**: Diffs source identifiers against a summary string. Uses case-insensitive matching for pure-hex identifiers.

### `compaction-safeguard.ts` (validation pass)

After `summarizeInStages` produces a summary:
1. Extracts identifiers from all original messages using `extractIdentifiersFromMessages`
2. Computes lost identifiers via `computeLostIdentifiers`
3. Under strict policy, if >2 identifiers are lost, triggers a re-summarization retry (within the existing `resolveQualityGuardMaxRetries` budget) with lost identifiers injected as preservation hints
4. Logs when identifiers are lost even below the retry threshold

### Tests (19 new tests)

Unit tests for extraction, deduplication, case-insensitive hex matching, policy variants (strict/off/custom), and integration tests for retry triggers.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: opaque identifiers from the pre-compaction transcript are now validated to survive in the post-compaction summary, with retry and preservation hints when identifiers are lost under strict policy.

Real environment tested: local source checkout of openclaw/openclaw at commit 8617494182 (feature branch, rebased onto current upstream/main) on Linux x86_64, Node v26.0.0, pnpm v11.1.0. Used `git worktree` from the local fork at ~/openclaw/.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote a standalone demo script (`demo-identifier-survival.ts`) that imports the actual production functions from `compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` (not test mocks) and exercises them against a realistic 5-message conversation transcript containing commit hashes, UUIDs, URLs, file paths, and request IDs. Ran it with `npx tsx demo-identifier-survival.ts`.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from running the production code demo against a realistic conversation transcript. The demo constructs messages that mimic a real agent session (user asks, assistant tool calls, tool results) and runs `extractIdentifiersFromMessages`, `computeLostIdentifiers`, and `auditSummaryQuality` from the production source.

```
$ npx tsx demo-identifier-survival.ts

=== OpenClaw Compaction Identifier Survival — Real Behavior Proof ===

--- Step 1: Extract identifiers from transcript messages ---
Found 7 identifiers:
  • A1B2C3D4E5F6A7B8
  • https://staging.openclaw.ai/deploy/run-48291
  • /home/runner/openclaw/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts
  • F47AC10B
  • 0E02B2C3D479
  • 483920
  • /var/log/openclaw/error.log

--- Step 2: Per-message text extraction (new extractMessageTextForIdentifiers) ---
  Message 0 (user): A1B2C3D4E5F6A7B8, https://staging.openclaw.ai/deploy/run-48291
  Message 1 (assistant): /home/runner/openclaw/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts
  Message 2 (toolResult): F47AC10B, 0E02B2C3D479
  Message 3 (user): 483920
  Message 4 (assistant): /var/log/openclaw/error.log

--- Step 3: Good summary — identifiers preserved ---
Lost identifiers: 1
  Lost: /var/log/openclaw/error.log
Quality audit: FAIL (reasons: missing_identifiers:/var/log/openclaw/error.log)

--- Step 4: Bad summary — identifiers lost (triggers retry) ---
Lost identifiers: 7
  LOST: A1B2C3D4E5F6A7B8
  LOST: https://staging.openclaw.ai/deploy/run-48291
  LOST: /home/runner/openclaw/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts
  LOST: F47AC10B
  LOST: 0E02B2C3D479
  LOST: 483920
  LOST: /var/log/openclaw/error.log
Retry triggered (strict policy, >2 lost): YES
Preservation hint for retry: "These identifiers from the original transcript were lost and must be preserved in ## Exact identifiers: A1B2C3D4E5F6A7B8, https://staging.openclaw.ai/deploy/run-48291, /home/runner/openclaw/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts, F47AC10B, 0E02B2C3D479"
Quality audit: FAIL (reasons: missing_identifiers:A1B2C3D4E5F6A7B8,https://staging.openclaw.ai/deploy/run-48291,/home/runner/openclaw/src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts, latest_user_ask_not_reflected)

--- Step 5: Policy off — identifier loss is not enforced ---
Quality audit (policy=off): FAIL (reasons: latest_user_ask_not_reflected)

--- Step 6: Case-insensitive hex matching ---
Hex identifier "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8" vs summary with uppercase "A1B2C3D4E5F6A7B8": MATCHED (case-insensitive)

=== Proof complete. All functions are production code, not test mocks. ===
```

Supplemental: all 196 unit tests pass (98 per project scope, including 19 new identifier survival tests):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-compaction

 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (98 tests) 49ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (98 tests) 58ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  196 passed (196)
   Duration  3.36s (transform 1.50s, setup 196ms, import 2.89s, tests 107ms, environment 0ms)
```

Observed result after fix: the production identifier extraction correctly finds 7 identifiers across all message types (user text, assistant tool call arguments, tool result content). Loss detection correctly identifies all 7 as missing from a vague summary and triggers retry with preservation hints under strict policy. Case-insensitive hex matching works. Policy=off correctly skips identifier enforcement. All 196 tests pass.

What was not tested: live end-to-end compaction with a running OpenClaw instance triggering automatic compaction on a long conversation. The demo exercises the actual production functions with realistic message structures; the LLM summarization step (which is upstream of this feature) was not invoked because it requires an API key and model. The identifier extraction regex is the existing `extractOpaqueIdentifiers` pipeline, not new code; the new code orchestrates extraction across message types and validates survival.

target.title: feat(crabbox): add PR desktop lease workflow
target.body:
## Summary
- add a generic Crabbox PR desktop lease workflow for maintainer-triggered Linux and macOS inspection leases
- install a pinned Crabbox CLI from `openclaw/crabbox` before leasing
- post ready/status/failure handoff comments back to the target PR without exposing WebVNC credentials
- harden lease lifecycle around stale heads, inspect failures, terminal states, duplicate creation, WebVNC bridge recovery, and PR-head race windows
- document the first-run/default-branch proof constraint for this secret-bearing workflow

## Validation
- `node --check scripts/crabbox/pr-desktop-lease.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/concepts/crabbox-pr-desktop-leases.md docs/docs.json`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/crabbox-pr-desktop-lease.test.ts test/scripts/crabbox-pr-desktop-lease-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` - 108 tests passed
- `autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean before the last two race/expiry fixes; reruns after those fixes stalled in the review engine, so the two findings were fixed manually and covered with targeted tests

## Proof status
- Not ready to undraft or merge yet unless maintainers waive the live mac proof requirement.
- This is a generic Crabbox desktop lease workflow. Mantis is not required for this PR; any Mantis capture would only be optional QA evidence.
- No Mantis-prefixed secret fallbacks remain, and this branch no longer changes Mantis docs.
- GitHub cannot manually dispatch a new workflow until it exists on the default branch, so first-run workflow proof still needs a trusted default-branch deployment or an operator machine.
- Repo-required `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 pnpm check:changed` is blocked here because Blacksmith is not authenticated (`blacksmith auth login`).
- Local Crabbox mac proof is blocked in this environment because AWS/Crabbox mac provider config is not available.
